full_text:
Heatwave alert across Rajasthan:Kota hottest, Barmer nights near 30°C, Jaipur records above-normal temperatures
- Copy link
The heatwave across Rajasthan intensified on Monday, with temperatures crossing 40°C in at least 12 cities. Kota emerged as the hottest location, recording a maximum of 42°C—higher than Barmer and Jaisalmer.
Barmer reported the warmest night, with the minimum temperature at 29.6°C. In Jaipur, the maximum temperature reached 40°C, about 3°C above normal levels.
The Meteorological Centre in Jaipur has issued a yellow alert, forecasting continued heatwave conditions and clear skies over the next few days.
Hot days, warmer nights worsen conditions
Cities like Jaipur and Phalodi are facing unusually high temperatures both during the day and at night. Minimum temperatures in these areas have remained above 26°C, making nights uncomfortable.
Phalodi recorded the highest night temperature at 28.4°C, followed by Barmer at 27.5°C. Jaisalmer, Kota, and Alwar also reported elevated night temperatures, reducing relief after sunset.
Jaipur sees above-normal temperatures
In Jaipur, the maximum temperature reached 40°C, while the minimum stood at 27°C—around 3 degrees above normal. Residents are facing heat stress throughout the day, with little respite at night.
Kota hospitals prepare for heatstroke cases
With temperatures peaking at 42°C, Kota has stepped up medical preparedness. Authorities have reserved a 200-bed pre-fabricated ward at the medical college, including a dedicated 50-bed unit for heatstroke patients.
Officials say the arrangement is aimed at handling a possible surge in heat-related illnesses.
Jodhpur, Ajmer, Udaipur see impact on daily life
In Jodhpur, temperatures have remained above 40°C for several days, with Monday recording 40.7°C. Streets are largely deserted during afternoon hours due to extreme heat.
Tourist activity has dropped sharply in Ajmer and Udaipur. Popular spots remain empty as visitors avoid stepping out in the harsh sun. Markets in Udaipur also saw reduced movement, with people seeking shade during peak hours.
Heatwave likely to persist in Alwar
Alwar recorded a high of 41.6°C, accompanied by hot winds and strong sunlight. The weather department has warned that heatwave conditions will continue for at least four more days, urging people to take precautions.
Slight relief in Sikar, but heat continues
Sikar saw a marginal dip in temperature due to winds, with the maximum recorded at 39°C. However, harsh sunlight and dry conditions continue to trouble residents.
Tourism hit, precautions advised
The rising temperatures have led to a decline in tourist numbers across major destinations. Authorities have advised people to avoid stepping out during peak hours, stay hydrated, and take necessary precautions as the heatwave shows no immediate signs of easing.
